projects.title: Potato Leaf Diseases Detection
projects.description: An AI-powered computer vision system for detecting and classifying potato leaf diseases using deep learning techniques. The system achieves 99.8% accuracy in disease detection and provides real-time analysis for farmers.
projects.longDescription: This project addresses a critical agricultural challenge by developing an intelligent system that can identify various potato leaf diseases with high accuracy. The system uses advanced computer vision techniques and deep learning models to analyze leaf images and provide instant disease diagnosis. This helps farmers take timely action to prevent crop loss and improve yield.
challenges: Handling diverse lighting conditions in field images
Managing large datasets of leaf images
Optimizing model performance for mobile deployment
Ensuring accuracy across different disease types
solutions: Implemented advanced image preprocessing techniques
Used data augmentation to improve model robustness
Optimized model architecture for mobile deployment
Created comprehensive training dataset with expert annotations
results: Reduced diagnosis time from hours to seconds
Successfully deployed on Hugging Face Spaces

projects.title: Full-Stack Authentication System
projects.description: A complete full-stack authentication system with React frontend and Express backend, featuring secure user registration, login, password reset, and session management.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive authentication system provides a secure and scalable solution for user management. Built with modern web technologies, it includes advanced security features like JWT tokens, password hashing, email verification, and role-based access control. The system is designed to handle high traffic and provides a seamless user experience.
solutions: Used bcrypt for secure password hashing
Implemented refresh token mechanism
Created session management system
Added comprehensive input validation

projects.title: Quick Sign - Digital Signature App
projects.description: A web-based digital signature application built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that allows users to create, customize, and save digital signatures with various tools and options.
projects.longDescription: Quick Sign is an intuitive digital signature application that provides a comprehensive solution for creating and managing digital signatures. The application features a responsive design, multiple drawing tools, signature customization options, and the ability to save signatures in various formats. It's perfect for businesses and individuals who need to create professional digital signatures quickly and easily.
solutions: Used HTML5 Canvas with optimized drawing algorithms
Implemented touch and mouse event handling
Created local storage system for signatures
Designed user-friendly interface with clear controls

projects.title: Comprehensive Dataset Collection
projects.description: A comprehensive collection of 15+ datasets spanning various domains including healthcare, entertainment, transportation, demographics, and more. All datasets are cleaned, processed, and ready for analysis.
projects.longDescription: This project involves curating, cleaning, and organizing datasets from various domains to create a comprehensive data repository. Each dataset is carefully processed to ensure quality, consistency, and usability. The collection includes datasets for machine learning, statistical analysis, and research purposes, making it valuable for data scientists, researchers, and students.

projects.title: QR-Code Generator
projects.description: A QR Code Generator built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript is a web application that allows users to input data (like a URL, text, or contact information) and instantly generate a scannable Quick Response (QR) code image.
projects.longDescription: A QR Code Generator built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript is a fast, client-side web tool. It uses HTML for the input structure and output display, and CSS for styling the user interface. The core functionality relies on JavaScript, which uses a dedicated library to encode the users data (like a URL or text) into a scannable QR code image displayed instantly on the page.

Providing intuitive customization and download features

encoded version of the original user input

projects.title: House Price Prediction - ML Regression
projects.description: A machine learning regression model that predicts house prices based on various features such as location, size, age, and amenities using advanced ML algorithms.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive house price prediction system uses machine learning regression techniques to estimate property values. The model analyzes multiple features including square footage, number of bedrooms/bathrooms, location, age, and market conditions. Various regression algorithms such as Linear Regression, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting are evaluated to identify the most accurate predictor. The project includes extensive data preprocessing, feature engineering, and cross-validation.
challenges: Dealing with outliers in price data
solutions: Applied data imputation techniques
Used StandardScaler for feature scaling
Implemented outlier detection and handling
Performed hyperparameter tuning with GridSearchCV
results: Random Forest achieved R² score of 0.92

projects.title: Diabetes Prediction Using Decision Tree
projects.description: A machine learning classification system using decision trees to predict diabetes risk based on medical and demographic features with interpretable results.
projects.longDescription: The Diabetes Prediction System uses decision tree classifiers to identify individuals at risk of diabetes. The model analyzes medical features such as glucose levels, blood pressure, BMI, age, and family history. Decision trees provide interpretable results that healthcare professionals can easily understand and act upon. The project includes data preprocessing, feature importance analysis, and model evaluation using precision, recall, and ROC curves.
solutions: Applied SMOTE for class balancing
Implemented privacy-preserving data handling
Used feature selection techniques
Created clear decision paths

projects.title: Chat Application with Real-Time Messaging
projects.description: A real-time chat application with instant messaging, user authentication, and a responsive interface built with React and Node.js.
projects.longDescription: A comprehensive chat application featuring real-time messaging capabilities. Built with React for the frontend and Node.js with Socket.io for real-time communication, the application provides instant message delivery, user presence indicators, and typing status. Includes user authentication, message history, and a clean, intuitive UI.

Created efficient database indexing
Deployed on scalable servers

projects.title: Heart Disease Prediction - Algorithm Comparison
projects.description: A comparative study of machine learning algorithms (SVM, KNN) for predicting heart disease risk with detailed performance analysis and evaluation metrics.
projects.longDescription: This project evaluates and compares multiple machine learning algorithms for heart disease prediction. Using a comprehensive medical dataset, the system trains Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) models to classify patients as at-risk or healthy. The project includes extensive data preprocessing, feature engineering, model comparison, and performance visualization using ROC curves and confusion matrices.
solutions: Applied SMOTE and resampling techniques
Performed parameter tuning using GridSearchCV
Standardized feature scaling for consistent results
Used cross-validation for robust performance evaluation
results: SVM selected as the best-performing model
ROC curves & confusion matrices included in evaluation

projects.title: Movie Recommendation System using Unsupervised Learning (DBSCAN)
projects.description: An intelligent movie recommendation system with an interactive web interface that uses unsupervised machine learning and DBSCAN clustering to group movies and recommend similar ones.
projects.longDescription: This Movie Recommendation System leverages unsupervised machine learning to recommend movies based on similarity patterns. Using DBSCAN clustering, the system analyzes factors such as genre, keywords, cast, rating, and user interaction metadata to automatically detect natural clusters of related movies—without requiring labeled data. Users can search for a movie, and the system suggests similar titles from the cluster. The project includes a modern web interface for smooth navigation and easy interaction.
challenges: Improving cluster purity for better recommendations
solutions: Used TF-IDF vectorization for movie metadata
Applied dimensionality reduction with PCA
Performed parameter optimization for DBSCAN
Filtered noise points to improve recommendation quality
results: Successfully generated movie clusters using DBSCAN
High-quality movie suggestions with unsupervised learning
Interactive search that returns relevant recommendations
Visualization of clusters for analysis and debugging

projects.title: Vehicle Part Failure Association using Unsupervised Learning
projects.description: An unsupervised machine learning system that identifies hidden associations between failing vehicle parts using clustering and association rule mining to improve maintenance efficiency.
projects.longDescription: This project analyzes vehicle repair and maintenance logs to identify patterns in part failures. Using unsupervised learning techniques such as Apriori-based association rule mining and clustering algorithms, the system uncovers which parts commonly fail together, recurring fault combinations, and potential root-cause failure chains. These insights help automotive service centers, manufacturers, and diagnostic tools to prioritize inspections, plan preventive maintenance, and reduce breakdown rates. The project also includes an interactive interface for visualizing part associations, cluster groups, and failure networks.
challenges: Sparse and inconsistent failure log data
solutions: Cleaned and transformed failure logs using one-hot encoding
Used Apriori for extracting strong associations
Applied clustering for grouping related failure sets
Visualized rule strength using scatter and network graphs
results: Generated actionable associations between part failures
Identified high-risk parts that frequently fail together
Improved failure prediction and maintenance planning
Cluster visualizations reveal clear failure patterns

projects.title: Titanic Survival Predictor
projects.description: A machine learning–based classification system that predicts whether a passenger would survive the Titanic disaster using demographic and travel data.
projects.longDescription: The Titanic Survival Predictor is a supervised machine learning project built using classification algorithms to estimate the survival probability of passengers aboard the Titanic. The model analyzes key features such as age, gender, ticket class, fare, family size, embarkation port, and cabin information. Multiple machine learning models—including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and KNN—are trained and evaluated to identify the most accurate predictor. The project includes feature engineering, data cleaning, and performance visualization with an intuitive prediction UI.
features: Data visualization of patterns and correlations
challenges: Handling missing age and cabin data
Dealing with class imbalance in survival labels
solutions: Imputed missing values using mean/median strategy
Used feature scaling and one-hot encoding
Trained multiple models with GridSearchCV tuning
Visualized correlations using heatmaps and distribution plots
results: Random Forest achieved the best accuracy of 90%
Generated survival predictions with high precision
Deployed with a simple web-based UI

projects.title: Sales Forecasting for a Retail Chain
projects.description: A machine learning and time-series forecasting system that predicts future retail sales using historical data, seasonality patterns, and advanced ML models.
projects.longDescription: Sales Forecasting for a Retail Chain is a predictive analytics project that uses machine learning and time-series forecasting methods to estimate future product sales. The system analyzes historical sales trends, seasonal fluctuations, promotional effects, and store-level patterns. Models such as ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM, and Random Forest Regression are evaluated to identify the best-performing forecasting technique. The project includes a dashboard-style web interface for visualizing trends, forecasts, and key insights—helping retail managers make data-driven business decisions.
features: Model comparison (ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM, Random Forest)
challenges: Handling missing and inconsistent sales data
Selecting the most accurate forecasting model
solutions: Applied data cleaning and imputation techniques
Used decomposition to analyze trend and seasonality
Performed hyperparameter tuning for forecasting models
Built a model comparison pipeline for accuracy evaluation
results: Generated weekly and monthly sales predictions
Interactive plots displaying trends and future values
Enabled better inventory and demand planning

projects.title: Portfolio Website
projects.description: A modern React.js portfolio website showcasing my journey as a Developer and Analyst, featuring interactive UI, animations, and dynamic project components.
projects.longDescription: The Portfolio Website is a fully-responsive and modern web application built using React.js to highlight my skills, projects, certifications, and professional experience. Designed with smooth animations, reusable components, and a clean UI/UX layout, the portfolio presents my journey both as a Software Developer and an Analyst. The website includes dynamic project data rendering, a contact form, and an elegant section-based navigation system.

projects.title: Skin Diseases Detection Using Deep Learning
projects.description: A deep learning–based image classification system for detecting 30 types of skin diseases using a large dataset of 15,000 dermatology images with labels in both English and Hindi.
projects.longDescription: The Skin Diseases Detection System is a deep learning project designed to identify various dermatological conditions from images. The dataset contains 34 unique skin disease classes with around 15,000 images, labeled in both English and Hindi. The system uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and transfer learning models such as EfficientNet, ResNet50, and MobileNetV2 to accurately classify skin conditions. The project includes image preprocessing, augmentation, hyperparameter tuning, and model evaluation using accuracy, precision, recall, and confusion matrices. A user-friendly web interface enables uploading an image and receiving real-time predictions.
features: High-accuracy CNN and transfer learning models
challenges: Large variation in image quality and lighting
High computational requirement for training CNNs
Ensuring generalization across different skin tones
solutions: Used advanced augmentation (rotation, zoom, flip)
Applied class balancing techniques and weighted loss

Performed hyperparameter tuning with learning rate scheduling

aim to achieve Real-time prediction with < 1.5 seconds processing
Visualization tools enabled better clinical interpretability

projects.title: My AI Advocate
projects.description: An AI-powered legal assistant and document management platform that provides instant legal information, document analysis, and secure storage using Google Gemini AI and Firebase.
projects.longDescription: My AI Advocate is a comprehensive AI-driven legal platform designed to assist users with legal information and document management. Built using React, Google Gemini API, and Firebase, the platform offers real-time AI chat for legal queries, AI-based document review, and secure cloud storage. Users can upload legal documents (PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT) and receive detailed AI-generated insights including contract analysis, compliance checks, and risk assessment. The system also tracks user activity, maintains chat and document history, and provides an admin panel for managing users, documents, and analytics. The application is fully responsive, secure, and designed with a professional legal disclaimer to ensure responsible usage.

Real-time chat interface with typing indicators

projects.title: Rainfall Prediction Using Linear Regression
projects.description: A machine learning project that predicts rainfall using Linear Regression. This project establishes relationships between weather variables (temperature, humidity, dew point) and precipitation to forecast rainfall amounts.
projects.longDescription: Predicting rainfall is a vital aspect of weather forecasting, agriculture planning, and water resource management. This project uses Linear Regression algorithm to establish relationships between dependent variable (rainfall) and independent variables (temperature, humidity, dew point) to predict how many inches of rainfall we can expect. The model analyzes multiple weather features and provides accurate precipitation forecasts for agricultural and hydrological planning.
features: Feature selection based on weather variables
Model evaluation with multiple metrics (MSE, RMSE, R²)
Visualization of predictions vs actual values

projects.title: Credit Card Fraud Detection - ML
projects.description: A machine learning-based web application to detect fraudulent credit card transactions using Random Forest Classifier trained on historical transaction data.
projects.longDescription: This project is a comprehensive machine learning solution for detecting fraudulent credit card transactions. It uses a Random Forest Classifier trained on a large dataset of historical transactions to classify new transactions as either fraudulent or normal. The project includes a full-stack implementation with a Flask web application for real-time fraud detection, model training and evaluation, and the ability to retrain the model with new data. The web interface provides an intuitive way to input transaction details and receive fraud predictions instantly.

Model persistence with joblib and pickle

projects.title: Customer Default Prediction using AdaBoost (LOAN)
projects.description: A machine learning model using AdaBoost ensemble learning technique to predict customer loan default risk. This project analyzes customer attributes to help banks assess creditworthiness and minimize credit risk.
projects.longDescription: Customer Default Prediction is a critical application used by banks and loan lenders to assess the creditworthiness of potential borrowers. This project implements a machine learning model using AdaBoost (Adaptive Boosting), an ensemble learning technique that combines multiple weak classifiers to create a robust and accurate strong classifier. The model analyzes various customer attributes including income, loan amount, home ownership, loan intent, and loan grade to predict whether a borrower is likely to default on their loan, helping financial institutions make informed lending decisions and minimize credit risk.
challenges: Achieving high prediction accuracy for credit decisions
solutions: Applied data preprocessing and imputation techniques
Used SimpleImputer for missing values
Implemented LabelEncoder for categorical variables
Tuned AdaBoost hyperparameters for optimal performance
Applied train-test split with cross-validation
results: Deployed on Flask web application for real-time predictions

projects.title: Box Office Revenue Prediction Using Machine Learning
projects.description: An interactive Flask web application that predicts movie box office revenue using an XGBoost regression model trained on historical movie data, with a beautiful responsive UI.
projects.longDescription: This project combines machine learning with a beautiful, responsive web interface to provide instant revenue predictions for movies. The application uses XGBoost, a state-of-the-art gradient boosting algorithm, trained on historical box office data. Users can input movie details including distributor, MPAA rating, opening theaters, theatrical release duration, and genres to receive instant revenue predictions. The application features real-time input validation, interactive UI with animations, and detailed results visualization with industry benchmark comparisons.

Log-scale processing for large revenue ranges

projects.title: E-Commerce Product Recommendations using CatBoost
projects.description: A machine learning-powered product recommendation system using CatBoost gradient boosting algorithm to predict customer interest in recommendations based on purchase behavior.
projects.longDescription: This project leverages CatBoost, a powerful gradient boosting algorithm, to build a product recommendation system for e-commerce platforms. The system analyzes customer purchase behavior using RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) analysis to predict which customers are likely to be interested in product recommendations. With over 90% accuracy, the model efficiently handles categorical data and includes built-in handling of missing values. The project features interactive GUI with real-time predictions using slider widgets, comprehensive visualizations, and model persistence for production deployment.

Implemented RFM analysis for meaningful features

projects.title: Hand Digit Recognition using CNN
projects.description: A deep learning web application that recognizes handwritten digits (0-9) using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). Built with TensorFlow/Keras and Streamlit, this app provides an interactive interface to draw or upload digit images for real-time predictions.
projects.longDescription: This hand digit recognition project implements a state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Network trained on the MNIST dataset to achieve 99%+ accuracy in recognizing handwritten digits. The web application, built with Streamlit, provides two interaction modes: users can either draw digits directly on an interactive canvas or upload images for recognition. The app features advanced preprocessing including automatic image inversion, noise reduction, morphological operations, and smart centering. Real-time predictions display confidence scores and probability distributions across all digits.
features: Interactive drawing canvas for digit input
Image upload functionality (PNG, JPG, JPEG, BMP)
Smart centering and aspect ratio preservation
Side-by-side original vs preprocessed image display
challenges: Handling different image orientations and lighting
Automatic detection of image polarity (dark/light digits)
Efficient memory usage for web application
solutions: Implemented image inversion detection algorithm
Used OpenCV for robust preprocessing pipeline
Optimized model architecture with dropout regularization
Trained on MNIST dataset (60k training, 10k test images)
Deployed on Streamlit for easy web access
results: Achieved 99%+ test accuracy on MNIST dataset
Sub-100ms inference time for real-time predictions
Handles both drawn and uploaded digit images
Robust preprocessing handles various image conditions

projects.title: Building Artificial Neural Networks from Scratch
projects.description: A comprehensive implementation of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) from scratch using only NumPy - no TensorFlow, PyTorch, or other deep learning frameworks!
projects.longDescription: This educational project demonstrates the fundamental principles of deep learning by building a fully functional Artificial Neural Network from the ground up. By implementing each component manually using only NumPy, learners gain deep insights into how neural networks process information, how backpropagation computes gradients, how gradient descent optimizes parameters, and the role of activation functions in learning. The project includes a complete implementation with initialization, forward propagation, cost computation, backpropagation, parameter updates, and training loops, demonstrated with practical examples like the AND gate logic function.
features: Pure NumPy implementation (no deep learning frameworks)
challenges: Computing gradients correctly for each layer
Understanding matrix calculus and chain rule
solutions: Implemented vectorized NumPy operations for efficiency
Used chain rule systematically for backpropagation
Applied proper weight initialization techniques
Tuned learning rate and epochs for convergence
Added detailed comments explaining mathematical operations
Tested with simple logic gates before complex tasks
results: Achieved 100% accuracy on AND gate classification
Reduced cost from 0.693 to 0.003 over 10,000 epochs
Comprehensive educational resource for deep learning fundamentals

projects.title: Expense Wallet - Smart Financial Tracking
projects.description: A modern, serverless expense tracking web application with zero hosting costs. Track expenses, investments, and salary seamlessly with Google Apps Script and GitHub Pages.
projects.longDescription: Expense Wallet is a sophisticated, serverless expense tracking application built with HTML5, CSS3, and Vanilla JavaScript, powered by Google Apps Script backend and Google Sheets database. The application features OTP-based authentication, real-time dashboard with summary cards, smart fuzzy search using Fuse.js, and complete expense management capabilities. With a cost of $0/month, it leverages free Google and GitHub services to provide a fully functional personal finance tracker accessible from any device.

Smart fuzzy search powered by Fuse.js
Automated CI/CD deployment via GitHub Actions

Implemented multi-level caching (RAM + Sheet-based)
Built client-side fuzzy search with Fuse.js
Created secure OTP verification system

Used localStorage for session management
Automated deployment with GitHub Actions CI/CD

Supports thousands of transactions (10M cell capacity)

projects.title: The Cat & Mouse Museum Heist
projects.description: A pursuit-evasion simulation game where you are the Guard. An AI intruder is loose in the museum. Catch it before it steals the artifact and vanishes using POMDP belief system and BFS pathfinding.
projects.longDescription: This is a turn-based pursuit-evasion game built on a 10×10 grid museum that combines classical AI search (BFS pathfinding) with probabilistic reasoning (POMDP belief updates) to simulate a realistic cat-and-mouse scenario. The guard (you) uses noisy sensor readings and belief maps to track and catch the AI intruder, who employs a sophisticated 4-mode threat-aware strategy. The game features real-time Pygame visualization with heatmap belief displays, sensor noise modeling, and dynamic AI behavior that switches between stealing, fleeing, and escaping modes based on proximity threats.
features: AI intruder with 4-mode threat-aware behavior (FLEE, HEIST, ESCAPE)
BFS pathfinding for both guard and intruder
POMDP belief system with real-time heatmap visualization
Noisy motion sensor with configurable false positive/negative rates
Museum world with walls, doors, exhibits, and artifact
Scoring system with intruder theft rewards
challenges: Implementing POMDP belief updates with noisy sensors
Balancing sensor noise for engaging gameplay
BFS pathfinding through maze with doors
solutions: Implemented belief diffusion with 12% spreading to neighbors
Created 4-mode AI behavior tree based on guard distance and artifact status
Applied realistic sensor noise model (80% detection, 10% false positive)
Built efficient BFS with wall/door awareness
Used Pygame heatmap overlay for belief visualization
Implemented threat distance calculation for mode switching
Added survival-over-loot strategy to AI behavior
results: Sophisticated AI that exhibits realistic escape behavior
Effective POMDP belief system tracks intruder probability
Demonstrable AI reasoning through observable behavior

projects.title: Classification Using Sklearn Multi-layer Perceptron
projects.description: Breast cancer classification using a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network implemented with scikit-learn. The model is trained on the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset to predict whether a tumour is malignant or benign, achieving 97-98% accuracy.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive machine learning project demonstrates breast cancer classification using a Multi-Layer Perceptron neural network. The MLP is a feedforward neural network capable of learning non-linear decision boundaries, making it well-suited for complex classification problems like medical diagnosis. The model is trained on the well-known Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset (569 samples, 30 features) and includes feature normalization, model evaluation with detailed metrics, and model persistence using joblib for later reuse.
features: Loads Wisconsin Breast Cancer dataset from scikit-learn
Splits data into training (80%) and test sets (20%)
Trains MLPClassifier with two hidden layers (64 → 32 neurons)
Evaluates accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score
Saves trained model using joblib for later reuse
solutions: Applied StandardScaler for feature normalization
Used train-test split for proper evaluation
Implemented adam solver with max_iter=1000
Set random_state=42 for reproducibility
results: High precision and recall for both malignant and benign classes
Excellent performance on medical classification task

projects.title: IPL Score Prediction using Deep Learning
projects.description: An interactive deep learning project to predict IPL innings scores from match context including teams, venue, batsman, bowler, runs, wickets, and overs. Built with Keras neural networks and ipywidgets for interactive predictions.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive deep learning project builds a regression model that predicts final IPL (Indian Premier League) cricket scores during an innings. The project includes complete data preprocessing pipeline with label encoding for categorical features, feature scaling using MinMaxScaler, and a Keras neural network trained to predict innings totals. The notebook features an interactive prediction widget that allows users to input match parameters and get real-time score predictions. The model analyzes historical ball-by-ball data to identify patterns in batting, bowling, venue characteristics, and match conditions.

projects.title: Prediction of Wine Type Using Deep Learning
projects.description: An end-to-end deep learning project to classify wine as Red or White using physicochemical features from the Wine Quality datasets. Built with TensorFlow and Keras for binary classification.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive deep learning project combines two wine quality datasets (red and white wine) and trains a neural network to predict wine type with high accuracy. The project includes complete data pipeline: loading CSV files with correct delimiters, adding type labels (1 for red, 0 for white), data exploration with visualization, and training a binary classification neural network. The model uses physicochemical features including acidity, sugar, chlorides, sulfur dioxide, density, pH, sulphates, alcohol content, and quality measurements to distinguish between red and white wines. The notebook includes dataset fixes, model architecture design, training configuration, and prediction capabilities.
features: Load red and white wine quality datasets
Wine type prediction on new samples
challenges: Handling CSV files with non-standard delimiters
solutions: Applied sep=';' for correct CSV parsing
Added type column before dataset concatenation
Built dense neural network with 2 hidden layers
Used appropriate batch size and epoch configuration
Implemented ReLU and Sigmoid activation functions

projects.title: Lung Cancer Detection using CNN
projects.description: A complete machine learning project featuring a Convolutional Neural Network for automated lung cancer detection from CT scan images, with a modern Flask web application for inference and prediction visualization.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive deep learning project combines a trained CNN model with a full-stack Flask web application for lung cancer detection from CT scan images. The model classifies lung tissue into three categories: Normal, Adenocarcinoma (most common lung cancer), and Squamous Cell Carcinoma (airway cancer). The application features drag-and-drop image upload, real-time predictions with confidence scores, probability distribution visualization, RESTful API endpoints, and responsive web design. The project includes comprehensive documentation with interactive HTML guides, Jupyter notebooks for model training, and production-ready deployment configurations.
features: Convolutional Neural Network with 3 Conv2D layers
Automated lung cancer detection from CT scans
Three-class classification (Normal, Adenocarcinoma, Squamous Cell Carcinoma)
Flask web application with REST API

projects.title: Cat & Dog Classification using Convolutional Neural Network
projects.description: A production-ready deep learning project with multiple frontend options for classifying images as cats or dogs using CNN. Features Flask web app and Streamlit interface for real-time predictions.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive deep learning project implements a Convolutional Neural Network for binary classification of images as cats or dogs. The project includes two production-ready frontend interfaces: a modern Flask web application and a Streamlit interface for data science workflows. The application features drag-and-drop image upload, real-time predictions with confidence scores, animated progress visualization, responsive design for desktop/tablet/mobile, and comprehensive error handling. The trained CNN model is optimized for 224×224 RGB images and provides instant predictions with confidence percentages. The project is deployed on Render for live production use and includes comprehensive documentation for both Flask and Streamlit interfaces.

projects.title: SSH Server with Twisted CDAC-Mohali
projects.description: A complete SSH server implementation using the Twisted framework with RSA key generation, secure key exchange, and remote command execution capabilities.
projects.longDescription: This project implements a fully functional SSH server using the Twisted asynchronous networking framework. The server features automatic 2048-bit RSA key generation and management, password-based authentication with secure Diffie-Hellman key exchange, and support for remote shell command execution. The implementation includes persistent connections that remain active until the client disconnects, comprehensive error handling, and detailed logging. The server listens on a configurable TCP port (default 2222) and supports multiple concurrent client connections. It includes pre-configured user credentials and can be easily customized for production environments.
features: RSA Key Generation: Automatic 2048-bit RSA public/private key pair generation
Secure Authentication: Password-based authentication with secure key exchange
Command Execution: Execute shell commands remotely via SSH
Persistent Connection: Connection stays active until client sends quit/exit
TCP Connection: Standard SSH protocol over TCP
Interactive Shell: Full interactive command prompt for continuous command execution
Multiple Users: Support for multiple user accounts with different passwords
Configurable Port: Easy port configuration for different environments
Detailed Logging: Complete server activity logging to file
Graceful Disconnection: Clean shutdown of connections
challenges: Implementing secure RSA key generation and management
Implementing command execution in secure environment
Ensuring proper encryption of all communication
solutions: Used Twisted SSH factory and protocol classes
Implemented RSA key generation with cryptography library
Applied Twisted's async architecture for concurrent connections
Used subprocess module with timeouts for command execution
Implemented heartbeat mechanism for connection persistence
Leveraged SSH protocol encryption built into Twisted

projects.title: Sentiment Analysis with a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)
projects.description: An end-to-end NLP project that classifies Swiggy customer reviews as Positive or Negative using a TensorFlow SimpleRNN model with Keras API.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive NLP project implements sentiment analysis on real-world customer reviews using a Recurrent Neural Network. The project takes Swiggy restaurant reviews and their ratings, preprocesses the text through cleaning and tokenization, converts reviews to padded sequences, and feeds them into a neural network with embedding and SimpleRNN layers. The model learns temporal patterns in text to accurately classify sentiments. The pipeline includes complete data preprocessing, label creation based on ratings, embedding generation, and binary classification with sigmoid activation. The Jupyter notebook provides cell-by-cell explanations of the entire workflow from data loading through model training and prediction on new reviews.
features: Training with 15 epochs and batch size 32
Custom prediction function for new reviews
solutions: Applied padding to standardize sequence length
Used SimpleRNN to capture sequential dependencies
Implemented binary crossentropy loss
Applied text cleaning pipeline (lowercase, punctuation removal)
Used word tokenization with vocabulary size 5000
Applied adam optimizer with appropriate learning rate
results: Working prediction function for new reviews

projects.title: Machine Learning Journey: Basic to Advanced
projects.description: Complete interactive ML repository covering the full journey from Python fundamentals to advanced deep learning, NLP, computer vision, and reinforcement learning with 26+ Jupyter notebooks.
projects.longDescription: This comprehensive educational repository is a complete learning guide for the entire machine learning journey from Python basics to advanced AI/ML/DL concepts. The repository contains 26+ interactive Jupyter notebooks organized into 9 progressive learning phases, each designed to build upon previous knowledge. It covers Python programming fundamentals, data manipulation with NumPy and Pandas, data visualization with Matplotlib/Seaborn/Plotly, statistical analysis, traditional machine learning algorithms, reinforcement learning, natural language processing, computer vision with object detection (YOLO), and annotation techniques. Each notebook includes detailed comments, practice exercises, real-world examples, and visual outputs. The learning path is structured for different audience levels (beginners, intermediate, advanced) with estimated time frames for each phase (8-12 weeks total for complete learning). All notebooks are designed to be interactive and hands-on, encouraging learners to code along and experiment.
features: Python basics to advanced deep learning
challenges: Balancing depth and breadth of topics
Providing hands-on exercises for each concept
Organizing large amount of content logically
Covering both theoretical and practical aspects
solutions: Organized into 9 sequential learning phases
Each notebook builds on previous knowledge
Included practice exercises and projects
Clear directory structure and roadmap
Detailed comments and explanations in code
Real-world examples and use cases
results: Support for complete beginner to advanced learner paths
